data.title: Nasdaq 100 Rebalancing Welcomes Five New Stocks
data.description:
On Thursday evening, Nasdaq (NDAQ) announced the results of its June 2026 quarterly rebalance. The rebalance will be effective ahead of the market open on Monday, June 22. Here are the five stocks that made the cut:

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

LABX: an alternative to margin or options on ALAB

  • Astera Labs (ALAB)
  • CoreWeave (CRWV)
  • Nebius (NBIS)
  • Rocket Lab (RKLB)
  • Teradyne (TER)

The Nasdaq 100 tracks 100 of the largest non-financial companies listed on the Nasdaq exchange. However, the index consists of over 100 stocks since Alphabet (GOOGL) has multiple share classes.

The Five Companies Leaving the Nasdaq 100

Only 100 companies are allowed in the index. Due to the new additions, five companies received the boot:

  • Charter Communications (CHTR)
  • Cognizant Technology (CTSH)
  • Insmed Incorporated (INSM)
  • Verisk Analytics (VRSK)
  • Zscaler (ZS)

To be eligible for inclusion, a company must have enough liquidity and public float while also ranking among the largest Nasdaq-listed companies by market capitalization.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Is Penny Stock Park Ha Biological Technology (BYAH) Up Today?
data.description:
Park Ha Biological Technology (BYAH) stock rocketed higher on Friday after the company announced a new strategic collaboration. The skincare solutions company's new partnership is with AI company Star Plus Action. The two companies intend to work together in the healthcare industry, skincare product development, celebrity IP management, and artificial intelligence technology. This is all with the goal of advancing the beauty and wellness sector.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Park Ha Biological Technology and Star Plus Action have already launched their first product together. This product is AI Nutritionist, which offers nutrition advice to users. The company also wants to release AI-powered store managers and AI-driven professional skincare specialists.

Park Ha Biological Technology's CEO said that the agreement "represents a pivotal upgrade for the brand, transitioning from a skincare-only focus to a comprehensive health and wellness ecosystem." The company intends to leverage its R&D, IP resources, and industrial expertise in combination with Star Plus Action's AI abilities to enhance product offerings and intelligent service systems. It argued this will allow it to create a differentiated core competitive advantage in the health and wellness industry.

Park Ha Biological Technology Stock Movement Today

Park Ha Biological Technology stock was up 153.33% in premarket trading on Friday, following a 15.32% fall yesterday. The stock has also decreased 86.79% year-to-date and 99.89% over the past 12 months.

Today's news brought heavy trading to BYAH stock, as some 47 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the company's three-month average daily trading volume is below 1 million shares.

Is Park Ha Biological Technology Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, Park Ha Biological Technology stock lacks significant analyst coverage. This leaves traders without guidance when handling the stock. However, they should note the stock's recent market movement. While the stock was up today, its recent falls should act as a red flag for potential investors. It has also had trouble with delisting warnings.
data.author: William White

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Forecast – NDX Inclusion, Strong Growth, and Mixed Analyst Views
data.description:
Shares of cloud infrastructure provider CoreWeave (CRWV) were up 4.9% in pre-market trading, following news of its inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 Index (NDX). Nasdaq (NDAQ) announced that it is adding five companies, including CoreWeave, Nebius (NBIS), Rocket Lab (RKLB), Astera Labs (ALAB), and Teradyne (TER) to the NDX, effective June 22.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

LABX: an alternative to margin or options on ALAB

Inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 is often a positive catalyst for a company. Index funds and ETFs tracking the index must buy the stock. This boosts demand for the stock, trading volume, and the share price in the short term. The changes are being made as part of Nasdaq's June quarterly rebalance.

CoreWeave's Financials Are Improving

CRWV stock has gained 33.7% so far in 2026. The rally has been driven by surging demand for AI infrastructure and GPU capacity. In Q1FY26, CoreWeave's sales increased by 127% year-over-year, with revenue hitting $2.08 billion. CoreWeave also saw the strongest bookings quarter in its history, with revenue backlog reaching $99.4 billion as of March 31.

Moreover, the company signed major new agreements with Meta (META), including a $21 billion commitment, and reached a multi-year deal with Anthropic to support the development of the Claude AI model.

Analysts Remain Divided Despite the Strong Rally

Analysts remain cautiously optimistic about CoreWeave's long-term outlook. Recently, Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Brett Knoblauch maintained his Buy rating and $167 price target (74.4% upside potential). He noted that CoreWeave has gained about 23% since the end of March, trailing many AI infrastructure peers, and called it a buying opportunity. He highlighted that CoreWeave has a much larger capacity than competitors, an expanding software platform, and a strong position to benefit from future AI demand.

On the other hand, Bernstein analyst Madison Rezaei reiterated his Sell rating on the stock, with a $67 price target (30% downside potential). He remains cautious on CoreWeave's long-term business outlook but does not see recent insider sales as a meaningful near-term concern. However, significant selling by major shareholders such as Jane Street or Nvidia (NVDA) could change that view.

Is CRWV a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, CRWV has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 13 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell rating. The average CoreWeave price target of $132.35 implies 38.2% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Dow Jones Climbs ahead of Highly-Anticipated SpaceX IPO
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Friday higher ahead of SpaceX's IPO at 9:50 Eastern Time. Shares of the spaceflight and telecommunications company are set to open at $135. That's equivalent to a $1.77 trillion valuation.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The IPO is expected to elevate Elon Musk's net worth to over $1 trillion. He currently owns over $270 billion in Tesla (TSLA) stock and options. The SpaceX IPO will likely provide with him another $841 billion.

Record SpaceX IPO Could Drive Market Volatility

SpaceX will raise a total of $75 billion by offering 555.6 million shares. 20% of the shares are allocated to retail investors. That's much higher than the typical 5% to 10%.

The IPO will be the largest of all time and could create volatility. This could occur due to investors selling out of other stocks to fund their SpaceX purchases. SpaceX is also considering bond sales, offering investors an alternative way to gain exposure without owning stock.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.description:

data.title: Intel (INTC) Is Transforming. The Stock Has Already Reacted
data.description:
Intel (INTC) confirms significant progress in its transformation from a product-only chipmaker into a combined chip designer and manufacturer. Accelerating demand for artificial intelligence (AI) and growing confidence in Intel's foundry ambitions have led to a 190% rally year-to-date. However, significant foundry losses, rising debt, weak cash generation, and a lofty stock valuation lead to question marks around INTC's risk/return profile. Therefore, I rate INTC stock Hold.

data.title: Billions at Stake as SEC Blocks ETF Providers from SpaceX IPO Day One Frenzy
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX), the rocket firm founded by Elon Musk, is preparing for a historic market debut as its shares are set to start trading on the Nasdaq (NDAQ) later today. However, asset managers hoping to launch leveraged exchange-traded funds (ETFs) tied to the stock on its first day of trading have been forced to delay their plans. The delay has caused a major setback for firms seeking to capitalize on the initial public offering (IPO) day-one trading frenzy.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

SEC Delay Blocks Leveraged SpaceX ETFs From Day-One Trading

According to reports, asset managers planning to launch leveraged ETFs linked to SpaceX have been instructed to postpone their products until Monday. The delay prevents traders from using leveraged funds to take advantage of SpaceX's expected first-day market activity.

Several sources familiar with the matter said exchanges informed issuers that the launches would need to be pushed back. This update came after Senator Elizabeth Warren and the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) raised some concerns about the IPO launch. The regulator wanted to avoid adding more pressure to what is currently one of the most closely watched stock market debuts in history.

Matt Markiewicz, Head of Product and Capital Markets at Tradr ETFs, said the firm had hoped to launch its 2x long and 2x short SpaceX ETFs on the same day the stock begins trading. Instead, the product launches are now postponed to Monday.

ETF Providers Risk Billions as They Prepare for SpaceX Trading Frenzy

The pushback comes as major ETF issuers, including Direxion, GraniteShares, ProShares, and Defiance, prepare to launch competing leveraged products tied to SpaceX. Many experts expect intense competition among ETF providers as investors seek to gain access to SpaceX's stock through various channels.

According to Strategas ETF analyst Todd Sohn, billions of dollars could flow into SpaceX-linked leveraged funds within the first few weeks of trading. Markiewicz also noted that the products could attract more than $10 billion in combined assets.

Asset managers had hoped that launching alongside SpaceX's market debut would give them a stronger edge in attracting early investor capital. However, the delay means firms will now have to wait until the first trading session after SpaceX's debut.

The development comes after SpaceX attracted more than $250 billion in share orders from large investors. The company has also successfully raised $75 billion at a valuation of almost $1.77 trillion.

Is SpaceX a Good Stock to Buy?

According to Oppenheimer (OPY) analyst Timothy Horan, SpaceX (SPCX) is rated a Moderate Buy. The stock is projected to skyrocket by up to 764.42%, with a 12-month price target of $190. Investors interested in the IPO can participate in the offering through several major brokerage platforms, although requirements may differ by platform. For more updates on SpaceX after its IPO, visit the TipRanks Stock Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Earns Credit Upgrade as S&P Sees 'Insatiable Demand' for AI
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA), the AI chip giant, received a credit-rating upgrade from S&P Global, citing "insatiable demand" for AI systems. S&P raised Nvidia's long-term issuer rating to AA from AA-, reflecting its growing confidence in Nvidia's role in the AI boom.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Explore NVDS for 2X short leverage on NVDA

S&P Expects Years of Strong Growth

S&P expects Nvidia's revenue to rise 82% to $394 billion in fiscal 2027. The agency then forecasts another 38% increase to $544 billion in fiscal 2028. S&P said demand for Nvidia's Blackwell chips remains strong and expects future products, including the Rubin platform, to help drive growth over the next few years.

Further, S&P said Nvidia's mix of AI chips and software helps set it apart from rivals like Advanced Micro (AMD). The firm believes that advantage allows Nvidia to maintain its lead and charge higher prices for its products.

Cash Flow Continues to Climb

The agency expects Nvidia's free cash flow to reach $196 billion in fiscal 2027 and $276 billion in fiscal 2028. Those figures could give the company even more room to fund share buybacks, dividends, and future growth.

During the most recent quarter, Nvidia approved an $80 billion share buyback program. The company also raised its quarterly dividend from $0.01 per share to $0.25 per share.

Risks Still Remain

Despite the upgrade, S&P highlighted several risks. Nvidia still relies heavily on TSM to make its most advanced chips. The agency also warned that that AI spending could slow if companies face funding challenges or if power shortages delay new data center projects.

Even so, S&P said it expects Nvidia remain a leader in AI and keep building its cash position.

Is Nvidia Stock Still a Buy?   

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 37 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average 12-month Nvidia price target of $311.41 per share implies 52% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Futures Rise on Potential U.S.-Iran Deal; All Eyes on SpaceX IPO
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rallied early Friday on hopes of a potential peace deal between the U.S. and Iran. President Donald Trump claimed that the U.S. "just made a great settlement of the war," subject to the "finalization of documents." Investors are upbeat about SpaceX's public listing on Nasdaq today. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.18%, 0.35%, and 0.56%, respectively, at 8:40 a.m. EDT on June 12.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

According to Iranian state media, the draft U.S.–Iran memorandum of understanding includes U.S.' commitments to lift oil sanctions, while Iran would reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices declined on expectations that tensions in the Middle East could ease. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 2.58% to $88.12 a barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) fell 2.7% to $85.35.

During Thursday's regular trading session, major U.S. indexes rebounded sharply after Trump called off planned strikes on Iran. The Nasdaq Composite gained 2.54%, while the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones advanced 1.75% and 1.86%, respectively.

Looking ahead, traders are focused on SpaceX's highly anticipated IPO today. Under the ticker symbol "SPCX," the company has priced its shares at $135 each, implying a market valuation of about $1.77 trillion.

Turning to trending stocks, Adobe (ADBE) stock fell more than 8% in Friday's pre-market trading after the software company's fiscal second-quarter adjusted operating margin fell short of expectations. The company also announced the exit of its CFO Dan Dunn on June 15.

Meanwhile, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock rose more than 2% after Citi upgraded its rating on the chip giant to Buy from Hold, citing upside in GPU demand.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
